revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Ikonahime no mikoto-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Shirahama|qid=Q11580003|1=Shirahama}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.693961|N|138.973669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{Expand Japanese|伊古奈比咩命神社}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Ikonahime no mikoto-jinja haiden.jpg }}
''''Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine''' (伊古奈比咩命神社) also known as Shirahama Shrine is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimoda City, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref name="mw-2023" /><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=日本吉 - NIPPON-KICHI|url=https://nippon-kichi.jp/article_list.do;jsessionid=0739B6DD868E22BEF516B29AFB353DAF?p=4186&ml_lang=en|access-date=2024-04-11|website=nippon-kichi.jp}}</ref>
It is the oldest shinto shrine in Izu.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/ Shirahamajinja: Earth-shaking spirits emanate where 3 tectonic plates meet {{!}} The Asahi Shimbun: Breaking News, Japan News and Analysis|url=https://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/14461443|access-date=2024-03-28|website=The Asahi Shimbun|language=en}}</ref>
It has a sacred mirror as its [[Shintai]]
It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref> It is also a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}, a high Rank of shrine in the old book [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]<ref name="mw-2023">{{Cite web |date=2023-06-05 |title=Ikonahime no mikoto shrine (Shirahama Shrine) - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/ikonahime-no-mikoto-shrine-shirahama-shrine/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605012318/https://shrineheritager.com/en/ikonahime-no-mikoto-shrine-shirahama-shrine/ |archive-date=2023-06-05 }}</ref>
It is dedicated to the deity Ikonahime no Mikoto, who holds a place, among the gods of the Izu Islands.<ref name="mw-2023" />
It is at the tip of the Izu Peninsula. It is recognized for its roots making it the oldest spiritual site in the region. The shrine pays homage to deities with a focus on Ikonahime no Mikoto and Mishima Daimyojin accompanied by Mime, Wakamiya and Tsurugi no Miko.<ref name=":0" />
== Origins and Legends == The shrines history dates back, to the year of {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|qid=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan's|1=Emperor Kōan}} reign when a deity was brought from Miyakejima. Initially enshrined in {{ill|Shirahama|en|Shirahama|qid=Q2545674|lt=Shirahama|1=Shirahama}} before being relocated to Mishima. Legend has it that 2,400 years ago Mishima Daimigami and other deities arrived at Shirahama to establish Izus territory.<ref name="mw-2023" />
Its establishment is credited to Mishima Daimyojin, a revered figure in Shinto lore. As the story goes Mishima Daimyojin arrived at the Izu Peninsula via the Kuroshio Current under guidance from his deities. Mime, Wakamiya and Tsurugi no Miko. He selected Shirahamas tranquil setting as his abode. The legend continues with Mishima Daimyojins union with Ikonahime no Mikoto from [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] lineage symbolizing a blend of beings and earthly sanctity.<ref name=":0" />
== Deities Honored == The primary deity worshiped at this shrine is Ikonahime no Mikoto, also recognized as the After The God of Mishima. Other revered deities include Mishima Daimyojin, Goddess Mime, Wakamiya and Tsurugi no Miko.<ref name="mw-2023" />
== Alleged Spiritual Blessings == The shrine is known for providing blessings such as protection from adversity fostering ties ensuring safety promoting bountiful harvests and business success safeguarding childbirth and traffic conditions, as well as enhancing household prosperity.<ref name="mw-2023" />
== Architectural Details == Documented in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] compiled in December 927 AD highlights its religious significance.<ref name="mw-2023" />
In the temple grounds there are 26 temples collectively referred to as Nijuroku Sha Shrine, established in 1921. These divine beings were originally revered individually in each home, within the shrines community.<ref name="mw-2023" />
== Pilgrimages and Festivities == The temple holds ceremonies and events that honor the historical and spiritual legacy of the Izu Islands. Visitors have the opportunity to participate in prayers and rituals showing respect to the deities and seeking their blessings.<ref name="mw-2023" />
=== The Hitachi Festival === One of the events, on Shirahama Shrines agenda is the lively Hitachi Festival held towards late October.<ref name=":0" />
The festival acts as a guiding light marking the beginning of the event of the year, across the Izu Seven Islands. The ceremonial rituals kick off with shrine priests lighting torches along the shoreline a gesture believed to alert all the deities residing on the Izu Seven Islands about the start of the festival. A branch from a tree is presented to the gods followed by a fireworks display that brightens up the night sky. As the torches fade away the grand finale comes with a fireworks show.<ref name=":0" />
The day after these initial festivities sees an influx of visitors at the shrine grounds. An assortment of stalls offering goods and services. The festival also includes performances by Shirahama Daiko, a drum ensemble and Sanbaso Dance. The latter has been designated as a asset by Shimoda City underscoring the festivals role in preserving and showcasing local cultural heritage.<ref name=":0" />{{Commons Category|Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja }}
==Gallery==
<gallery> File:Ikonahime no mikoto-jinja haiden.jpg|alt=拝殿|Haiden (Worship Hall) of Ikonahime no Mikoto-jinja File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Daimyojin-iwa-2.JPG|alt=大明神岩-2|Daimyojin-iwa 2 (Great deity Rock) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Daimyojin-iwa.JPG|alt=大明神岩|Daimyojin-iwa (Great deity Rock) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Hakuryu-no-byakushin.JPG|alt=白龍の白榊|Hakuryu-no-byakushin (White Sacred Tree of the White Dragon) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja honden-1.JPG|alt=本殿-1|Honden 1 (Main Shrine) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja honden-2.JPG|alt=本殿-2|Honden 2 (Main Shrine) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Junimyojin-sha.JPG|alt=十二明神社|Junimyojin-sha (Twelve Deities Shrine) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja keidaisha.JPG|alt=境内社|Keidaisha (Auxiliary Shrine) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Mikama Entrance.JPG|alt=御鎌入口|Mikama Entrance File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Mime-benzaiten.JPG|alt=御銘弁財天|Mime-benzaiten (Engraved Benzaiten) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja shasou.JPG|alt=社倉|Shasou (Shrine Storehouse) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Sunamura-inari-jinja.JPG|alt=砂村稲荷神社|Sunamura-inari-jinja File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja torii.JPG|alt=鳥居|Torii (Shrine Gate) File:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja Yakushi-no-byakushin.JPG|alt=薬師の白榊|Yakushi-no-byakushin (Medicinal deity's White Sacred Tree) File:Ikonahimenomikotojinja.jpg|alt=伊古奈比咩命神社一望|A broad view of Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja File:IkonahimenoMikotoJinja2.jpg|alt=伊古奈比咩命神社別の視点|Another view of Ikonahimeno Mikoto Jinja File:Shirahama Shrine 白浜神社 下田市.jpg|alt=白浜神社|Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine in Shimoda City File:伊古奈比咩命神社境内出土 祭祀遺物.JPG|alt=境内出土祭祀遺物|Ceremonial artifacts excavated within Ikonahime no Mikoto-jinja precinct </gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|lt=Shikinai Taisha|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]] * [[Beppyo Shrine]]
== References == {{reflist}}

}}
}
{{translated page|ja|伊古奈比咩命神社|version=103424562|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--all this actually mandates is a completion tier 7 ranking. We are not using the promising draft template here. If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine--><!--Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine--><!--Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine--><!--Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine (Q11379183)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kamo District, Shizuoka|en|Kamo District, Shizuoka|ja|賀茂郡|ar|مقاطعة كامو (شيزوكا)|ceb|Kamo-gun (distrito sa Hapon, Shizuoka-ken)|fa|ناحیه کامو، شیزوئوکا|fr|District de Kamo (Shizuoka)|id|Distrik Kamo, Shizuoka|it|Distretto di Kamo (Shizuoka)|ko|가모군 (시즈오카현)|nl|District Kamo (Shizuoka)|pl|Powiat Kamo (Shizuoka)|ru|Камо (Сидзуока)|uk|Повіт Камо (Сідзуока)|zh|賀茂郡|lt=Kamo district|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}} || {{ill|Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine|lt=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine|ja|伊古奈比咩命神社|commons|Category:Ikonahimenomikoto-jinja|fr|Ikonahime no Mikoto-jinja|qid=Q11379183|1=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.694053|138.973614|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181572 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11379183}}==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[da:d:Q11379183]] [[ja:伊古奈比咩命神社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimoda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimoda]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shizuoka Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
